key.name: disable-qos
key.description: By default the Wi-Fi Display video encoder is throttled (frames are dropped) when encoded frames fall behind, in order to keep latency low. Some receivers have weak decoders that stall and never recover once the stream has been throttled, leaving the picture frozen. Enabling this option disables that throttling, so a full-rate stream with regular keyframes keeps flowing and such receivers can resync — at the cost of higher latency on congested links.
